prompt: Create a ForgeFlow Work Orchestration platform that helps field teams and office coordinators manage service requests, task execution, and supporting documentation entirely on-device. The app supports three roles: Administrator (sets policies, storage limits, and approval gates), Coordinator (posts requests, schedules work, and manages assignments), and Technician (accepts work, executes tasks, and submits evidence). Users sign in with a local username/password (minimum 10 characters, at least 1 number) and can unlock with FaceID/TouchID via LocalAuthentication; a 5-minute inactivity timeout re-locks sensitive screens. Coordinators create a service posting with a title, site address, due date/time (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour), and a budget cap (e.g., $2,500.00), then invite one or more Technicians or allow open acceptance; acceptance is idempotent so repeated taps never create duplicate assignments, and conflicts are resolved by "first accepted wins" with a visible audit note. Each posting automatically generates a task breakdown with subtasks, dependencies, priority (P0–P3), and progress states (Not Started/In Progress/Blocked/Done); blocked tasks require a comment. A calendar view and a to-do center provide daily routing, with iPhone and iPad layouts adapting to portrait/landscape and iPad Split View, plus Dark Mode support and Dynamic Type scaling. Comments support attachments and inline images; in-app notifications are required, while email/SMS/other channels are represented as extensible "connectors" that remain disabled offline. Notifications must deduplicate by (event type + posting ID + recipient) within 10 minutes, honor do-not-disturb hours, and show delivery/seen status in a local Messaging Center. All data persists locally using GRDB over SQLite with async/await for responsive UI and background work, implemented in SwiftUI with Safe Area and accessibility-first navigation. Core models include User, Role, ServicePosting, Assignment, Task, Dependency, Comment, Attachment, Notification, and PluginDefinition. Attachments are stored in the iOS app sandbox with quota controls (default 2 GB per user, 30-day cleanup for orphaned files) and strict validation: allowlist types (PDF, JPG, PNG, HEIC, MOV), max 250 MB per file, and basic format fingerprint checks (magic bytes) to reject disguised files; no cloud antivirus is used. Images are compressed on-device to a max 2048 px long edge and thumbnails are generated for lists; watermarking/masked-download is supported as an optional per-posting policy by rendering a stamped preview while keeping the original encrypted at rest. Upload/download is unified for local imports, with resumable chunking for files over 50 MB and retry with exponential backoff under weak connectivity even though the app remains fully usable offline. Offline capability includes tiered caching (keep last 90 days of postings and all "Active" assignments pinned), incremental sync logic scoped to device-to-device transfers via local file export/import, consistency validation (hash-based), and a sync status screen; heavy processing (compression, chunking, cleanup) runs via Background Tasks with battery-sensitive throttling and graceful handling of memory warnings. An extensible plugin mechanism lets Administrators define new component categories and custom fields/rules (e.g., ARGB lighting header count, PCIe generation, M.2 keying, cooler height limit in inches), test them in a local sandbox against sample postings, and require a two-step release approval workflow before rules can be applied to live postings.
